title: Nix Packaging Standard — .github/update.json
description: Config consumed by the Nix Packaging Standard update.sh. Canonical repo: github.com/Daaboulex/nix-packaging-standard.

description.description: Canonical GitHub repo description, applied by sync-meta.sh. Convention: '<Upstream Name> packaged for NixOS — <one concise clause>' (HM-only: '… Home Manager module — …'; original projects describe directly). No cross-repo references, no marketing fluff, one clause.

topics.description: Canonical GitHub repo topics, applied by sync-meta.sh. Convention: 'nix', 'nixos', 'flake' baseline + 'nixos-module'/'home-manager' where applicable + the software name + a few domain topics. Lowercase, hyphenated.

platforms.description: Documented exceptions to the fleet's canonical architecture set (x86_64-linux + aarch64-linux). The SUPPORTED set is the flake's `systems` — the executable truth CI builds; this field carries only the REASON each canonical arch the repo does NOT build is dropped. Absent: the repo builds the full canonical set. fleet-audit binds the two — a canonical arch missing from `systems` with no reason here is a silent drop (RED); a reason for an arch the flake DOES build is stale (RED).

aarch64-linux.description: One-line reason this repo does not build aarch64-linux (e.g. 'amd64-only upstream .deb', 'x86-only asm in upstream Rust', 'no pkgsi686Linux on aarch64'). 'untested' is not a reason — declare it in `systems` and let the arm runner verify instead.

tagFilter.description: ERE matched against release/tag names (github-release, github-tag). Selects the newest matching tag — pin to one namespace when upstream publishes several (e.g. "^[0-9]" to skip "android/*"). Default ".*" matches all.

packageFile.description: File `nix build .#default` centers on. null/omitted for module-only or non-building repos (upstream type none/trackOnly).

versionFile.description: File holding the canonical version literal (default: packageFile).

versionAttr.description: Attribute name to match — handles `version = "x"` and parameterized `<attr> ? "x"`.

revFile.description: File holding the package src `rev` literal for commit-tracked upstreams (default: versionFile). Set it when several `rev = "..."` literals exist so the package src rev is bumped, not a dependency's.

versionScheme.description: How the version literal is written. 'literal': the upstream string verbatim (a bare 7-char SHA for commit-tracked repos). 'unstable-date': '<versionBase>-unstable-<YYYY-MM-DD>' (nixpkgs VCS-snapshot convention) — commit-tracked types only; comparison is by rev. 'rev-only': bump rev (+ hash + date) and LEAVE the human-set version string untouched (git-snapshot packages like mesa-git whose version is upstream's self-reported MESA_VERSION); commit-tracked types only; comparison is by rev.

versionBase.description: Base version prefix for versionScheme 'unstable-date' (e.g. last release '2.0.0'). If omitted, derived by stripping any '-unstable-*' suffix from the current version.

trackOnly.description: Hand-mirrored repos with no buildable artifact: the updater only DETECTS upstream movement (against trackFile/trackKey) and files a 'remirror-needed' reminder issue — it never writes, builds, or advances a version. Requires a commit-tracked upstream type.

trackFile.description: trackOnly: JSON file recording the last-mirrored upstream marker.

trackKey.description: trackOnly: key in trackFile holding the recorded upstream commit.

hashes.description: SRI hash fields in evaluation-dependency order (source first).

oneOf.description: Bare field name — auto-located in the first *.nix file declaring it.

Explicit placement when a field name appears in multiple files. `file` may be a .json (e.g. version.json) for a JSON-resident hash ("field": "sha256-..."); required there since the bare-string form only scans *.nix.

binary.description: Binary under result/bin to run; null to skip.

check.description: Artifact check when no binary is run. 'eval': eval + clean build are the verification, no artifact-shape assertion (also the behavior when omitted). 'elf'/'wrapper'/'desktop': additionally assert the named artifact exists under result/.
